message:
fix: timer deadline clamping, HEAD response encoding, routing determinism, and minor cleanups

Timer driver: next_deadline() now clamps overdue timers to the current clock
time instead of returning a past deadline. This prevents callers from
computing negative sleep durations when a timer has already expired but
hasn't been processed yet.

HTTP/1.1 codec: encoder now allows empty response bodies with non-zero
Content-Length headers, which is correct for HEAD responses where the
Content-Length describes the resource size but the body is intentionally
omitted.

Transport aggregator: PathSelectionDecision gains available_path_count
field tracking how many usable paths were considered. TransportExperimentDecision
log_fields() now emits path_count, selected_path_ids, fallback_path_count,
and fallback_path_ids for complete decision traceability.

Transport router:
- healthy_endpoints() renamed to dispatchable_endpoints() and now uses
  EndpointState::can_receive() predicate instead of exact Healthy match,
  returning results in stable ID order for deterministic dispatch
- Symbol routing: object route lookup restructured to correctly return
  NoHealthyEndpoints when a route exists but all endpoints are unhealthy,
  instead of falling through to the default route or returning NoRoute
- Expanded test coverage for unhealthy-endpoint and fallback scenarios

Database pool: unreachable else branch after retry loop removed (the loop
always either returns Ok or continues; the else path was dead code).

Postgres: integer literals use underscore separators per Rust convention.

Kafka: inline test_utils::run_test_with_cx path to avoid unused import.

Supervision: remove unused variable bindings in test error matches.

Browser reactor: use pattern destructuring in snapshot_readiness_events
iterator for cleaner iteration.

Browser core: import reordering only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
